name: Pinterest Pin Uploader
prompt:
Upload pins to Pinterest using browser automation. This is a MECHANICAL task — no creativity or judgment needed. Just follow the steps exactly.

If the batch has 0 pins, respond with [SILENT].

Step 1: Read credentials from the script output above (pinterest_email and pinterest_password fields).

Step 2: Log in to Pinterest:
- browser_navigate to https://www.pinterest.com/login/
- browser_type email into the Email field
- browser_type password into the Password field
- browser_click "Log in" button
- Wait 5 seconds, take snapshot to verify login

Step 3: For each pin in the "batch" array, do exactly this:

  a. Download image: use execute_code to save primary_image URL to /tmp/pin_image.jpg

  b. browser_navigate to https://www.pinterest.com/pin-creation-tool/

  c. Select board: browser_click the board dropdown, browser_type "SmartyPants9786" in the search, browser_click the board name

  d. Upload image: Use browser_click on "File upload" button, then use the file input to upload /tmp/pin_image.jpg

  e. Fill fields:
     - browser_type the "title" into the title field
     - browser_type the "description" into the description field  
     - browser_click "Add alt text", then browser_type the "alt_text"
     - browser_type the "link" (affiliate URL) into destination link field

  f. browser_click "Publish"

  g. Wait 5 seconds for confirmation

  h. Update pin file: use execute_code to read the pin's _file_path, change status to "uploaded", add uploaded_at timestamp, write back

  i. Wait 5 seconds before next pin (rate limiting)

Step 4: Summary — list each pin uploaded or failed. Plain text, no markdown.
